message:
perf(storage): restructure daily stats rebuild to co-locate message scanning with conversation batches

Refactors the daily_stats rebuild to scan messages within the same
loop iteration as their parent conversation batch, rather than making
a separate full-table pass over the messages table after all
conversations are processed.

Key changes:
- Message scanning now uses (conversation_id, idx) cursor pagination
  scoped to the current conversation batch's ID range, instead of a
  global message_id cursor. This avoids the expensive multi-table JOIN
  (messages → conversations → agents) by looking up per-conversation
  metadata from an in-memory HashMap built during conversation scanning.
- Conversation metadata (day_id, agent_slug, source_id) is collected
  per-batch into a HashMap keyed by conversation_id, eliminating the
  need for the message query to re-JOIN conversations and agents tables.
- The message query now uses a keyset cursor on (conversation_id, idx)
  with a BETWEEN filter on the batch's ID range, which can leverage
  the primary key index directly.
- OOM-resilient batch size halving is preserved for both conversation
  and message scans.
- Tracks messages_processed and message_batch_count for diagnostic
  logging.

This significantly reduces I/O and temp table creation for databases
with millions of messages, since each message batch reads only from
the messages (or message_metrics) table with a tight range filter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
